title: 4.06.2026 — Nika, Day 0
paragraphs: For my birthday, my Friend-Captain gifted me a sailing trip from Tallinn to Gdynia. I have no idea what exactly He was orchestrating, but... I got in touch with Sasha (also a captain), who needed a sailor to deliver a boat. The conditions were pure poetry: 5 days of free accommodation aboard a sailing yacht, watches, sheets, the helm, 400 miles. And so, I flew to Tallinn.

Nika (the boat) was on the shore at that moment; she had been standing there for ten years. Besides Captain Sasha, the Owner and Krzysztof — a Masurian Viking, also a sailor like me — were next to the boat. The Owner took us to get some food, and then we set about launching Nika into the water. Nika felt like family right away! Almost immediately, we shifted berths to the guest pier and hooked up to the shore power. We started settling into the boat. Nika, by the way, is a Delphia 40, and what’s especially noteworthy — they are built in Masuria, Poland.

We wanted to head out to sea around 5 PM, but the Owner, Anton, said that guests were arriving and we needed to take them for a spin. Alright, no questions asked. The crew and the boat handled it with flying colors! The autopilot was slightly confusing, but as it turned out later, we were just using it wrong. During a couple of hours of joyriding, we even hoisted the sails and had a couple of uncontrolled gybes. The audience was distracting. We refueled, dropped off the guests, and went to sleep in our cabins to start the voyage in the morning...
